# nika.sh — llms.txt
# https://llmstxt.org/

> Nika is an open workflow language for AI — plus its reference engine in Rust.
> Apache-2.0 spec · AGPL-3.0-or-later engine. Paris. SuperNovae Studio.

## What

Nika reads `.nika.yaml` files and executes them as AI workflows.
Four verbs, locked forever: `infer` (LLM call), `exec` (subprocess),
`invoke` (builtin/tool — HTTP fetch is the `nika:fetch` builtin here),
`agent` (agent loop). Everything callable is a tool under `invoke`;
a verb is a distinct native execution model.

The language envelope is `nika: v1` — frozen forever. The engine versions
independently (forever-v0.x semver; no v1.0 ever).

Stdlib v0.1: 22 builtins · 14 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini,
Mistral, Groq, DeepSeek, xAI, OpenRouter, Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp,
LocalAI, vLLM, mock) · 9 extract modes. Bring your own keys — every key
stays yours. Local-first. Runs on your laptop, no daemon.

## Links

- Language spec (Apache-2.0): https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec
- Engine source (AGPL): https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika
- Install: https://nika.sh/#install (or `brew install supernovae-st/tap/nika`)
- Docs: https://docs.nika.sh
- Releases: https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika/releases

## Status

Chrysalis (pre-v0.90 emergence). 13 of 42 organs admitted — each passes
a 12-gate admission (TDD, mutation testing ≥90%, zero `.unwrap()` in src).
Language v0.1 public spec + the first usable binary (v0.81): summer 2026.
Building in public.

## License

Spec: Apache-2.0 (adopt freely, patent grant). Engine: AGPL-3.0-or-later.
Full copyleft. No SaaS loophole.
